prompt: The indexer panics when processing files with non-UTF8 filenames

turns.content:
<task>
The indexer panics when processing files with non-UTF8 filenames. Fix it to skip or handle these files gracefully.
</task>

arguments.reasoning: Line 10: `path.to_str().unwrap()` will panic if the path contains non-UTF8 bytes (common on Linux with certain locales or when files were created by other programs). The fix is to use `to_string_lossy()` for best-effort conversion, or skip the file. Since this is an indexer, lossy conversion is reasonable — the filename will be mostly correct and the file won't be silently dropped.

arguments.query: rust Path to_str non-utf8 to_string_lossy vs skip best practice

turns.content:
1. Path handling in Rust - non-UTF8 - https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/path/struct.Path.html#method.to_string_lossy
   to_string_lossy() replaces invalid UTF-8 with U+FFFD replacement character.
2. Rust OsStr and non-UTF8 paths - https://stackoverflow.com/questions/37388107
   Use to_string_lossy for display, to_str for strict validation.

arguments.title: Rust non-UTF8 path handling
arguments.content:
Path::to_str() returns None for non-UTF8 paths → unwrap panics.
Path::to_string_lossy() replaces invalid bytes with U+FFFD (safe, no panic).
Best practice: use to_string_lossy() for display/indexing, to_str() + match for strict validation.

turns.content: Finding recorded: Rust non-UTF8 path handling

arguments.message: Found the panic: `path.to_str().unwrap()` on line 10 of src/scanner.rs. This crashes on non-UTF8 filenames. I'll use `to_string_lossy()` which replaces invalid bytes with a replacement character — this way no files are silently skipped and the indexer stays robust.

arguments.summary: Fixed panic by replacing path.to_str().unwrap() with path.to_string_lossy().into_owned(). Non-UTF8 filenames now get replacement characters instead of crashing.
arguments.final_answer: Fixed the panic in src/scanner.rs line 10. Replaced `to_str().unwrap()` with `to_string_lossy().into_owned()` which safely handles non-UTF8 filenames by substituting invalid bytes with U+FFFD.
